lines.text: Hi! I'm Nova. We're going to play with a chocolate bar and find something a little bit magic hiding inside it. Ready?
We'll cut a chocolate bar into pieces and find out when two different fractions are secretly the exact same size. It's quick, and it's pretty cool. Want to try?
This is half a chocolate bar — one piece out of two. Give it a tap to smash it into smaller pieces. Go on, try it!
Whoa! You cut it into more pieces — but look closely: the chocolate part didn't get any bigger or smaller. Same amount, just smaller cuts. Smash it again if you like, then tap Continue.
Here's the secret. When you cut it more, the number changes but the amount stays exactly the same. One half and two quarters are the same size. Fractions like that have a name: equivalent.
Look at where the gold ends. The top bar — one half. The bottom bar — two quarters. They stop at the exact same line. Same amount of chocolate. That is what equivalent means.
Your turn! Make the bottom bar show the same amount as the half on top. Tap a quarter to fill it in — one at a time — and watch what happens.
Yes! Two quarters fills exactly the same as one half. They are equivalent — and you just built it yourself.
Quick question. Which one of these is the same amount as one half?
Ooh, not quite — and that's okay, look with me. One out of three only reaches up to here. One half reaches further. That's less chocolate, so it can't be the same. Try another one!
So close! Three out of four reaches past the half line — that's more chocolate than one half. We want exactly the same amount. Give it another go!
Exactly right! Two out of four is the same as one half. You really get this now.
True or false? Three out of six is also the same as one half. Look at the bars and decide.
Take one more look. Three out of six fills the bar right up to the same line as one half. So it really is the same amount. Want to call it again?
One big question. We did all that with halves. Does the same trick work for other fractions too? Here's one third — give it a smash and let's see.
Look at that! One third turned into two sixths — and the chocolate is still exactly the same amount. The trick is not just for halves. It works for every fraction.
So tell me — is one third the same amount as two sixths? Check the bars.
Peek at the bars — the gold ends at the very same line for both. So one third and two sixths really are the same amount. Want to call it again?

You did it! One half is two quarters is three sixths. One third is two sixths. Cut every piece into more pieces and the amount never changes — and that works for any fraction. You did not just learn a fact, you found the rule. Awesome work.
Tap the menu to choose a game.
Hold the tap to pour water into the glass. Stop at the line!
Almost — that's a little under. Try again.
Whoa, that's over the line. Have another go.
Perfect! That's exactly where we wanted it.
Tilt the iPad to pour the water out. Tilt it back when you reach the line.
Touch the button to let me listen for the tilt.
Swipe across the cake to slice it. Aim to cut off the target fraction.
Nice swipe! Now check the slice.
Hmm, that piece is a little too small. Slice again?
That piece is a little too big. Slice again?
You sliced it exactly right. Beautiful work.
Ready for the next one?
Tap anywhere to begin.
That's exactly one half of a glass. Beautiful. Ready for the next?
A little too much for one half. Tap empty and have another go.
Just under one half. Tap empty and try again.
Stop at one half…
Press and hold the button below. Pour the glass up to one half.
That's exactly three quarters of a glass. Beautiful. Ready for the next?
A little too much for three quarters. Tap empty and have another go.
Just under three quarters. Tap empty and try again.
Stop at three quarters…
Press and hold the button below. Pour the glass up to three quarters.
That's exactly one third of a glass. Beautiful. Ready for the next?
A little too much for one third. Tap empty and have another go.
Just under one third. Tap empty and try again.
Stop at one third…
Press and hold the button below. Pour the glass up to one third.
That's exactly two thirds of a glass. Beautiful. Ready for the next?
A little too much for two thirds. Tap empty and have another go.
Just under two thirds. Tap empty and try again.
Stop at two thirds…
Press and hold the button below. Pour the glass up to two thirds.
That's exactly one quarter of a glass. Beautiful. Ready for the next?
A little too much for one quarter. Tap empty and have another go.
Just under one quarter. Tap empty and try again.
Stop at one quarter…
Press and hold the button below. Pour the glass up to one quarter.
You poured every single one. You really feel the fractions now.
Perfectly poured — one half left in the glass. Onward!
Too much left — you needed only one half. Tap fill and try again.
You poured a bit too far. Aim to leave one half. Tap fill and retry.
Tap "Listen for tilt", then tilt the iPad to pour. Leave one half behind.
Drag the slider to tilt the glass. Stop pouring when one half is left.
Tilt to pour — stop when the water reaches one half.
Perfectly poured — one third left in the glass. Onward!
Too much left — you needed only one third. Tap fill and try again.
You poured a bit too far. Aim to leave one third. Tap fill and retry.
Tap "Listen for tilt", then tilt the iPad to pour. Leave one third behind.
Drag the slider to tilt the glass. Stop pouring when one third is left.
Tilt to pour — stop when the water reaches one third.
Perfectly poured — two thirds left in the glass. Onward!
Too much left — you needed only two thirds. Tap fill and try again.
You poured a bit too far. Aim to leave two thirds. Tap fill and retry.
Tap "Listen for tilt", then tilt the iPad to pour. Leave two thirds behind.
Drag the slider to tilt the glass. Stop pouring when two thirds is left.
Tilt to pour — stop when the water reaches two thirds.
Perfectly poured — one quarter left in the glass. Onward!
Too much left — you needed only one quarter. Tap fill and try again.
You poured a bit too far. Aim to leave one quarter. Tap fill and retry.
Tap "Listen for tilt", then tilt the iPad to pour. Leave one quarter behind.
Drag the slider to tilt the glass. Stop pouring when one quarter is left.
Tilt to pour — stop when the water reaches one quarter.
Perfectly poured — three quarters left in the glass. Onward!
Too much left — you needed only three quarters. Tap fill and try again.
You poured a bit too far. Aim to leave three quarters. Tap fill and retry.
Tap "Listen for tilt", then tilt the iPad to pour. Leave three quarters behind.
Drag the slider to tilt the glass. Stop pouring when three quarters is left.
Tilt to pour — stop when the water reaches three quarters.
You poured every one. You're reading fractions like a chef.
You sliced exactly one half of the cake. Magnificent. Next?
Close! That slice is a bit too big for one half. Slice again.
Close! That slice is a bit too small for one half. Slice again.
Swipe down across the cake along the dotted line to slice off one half of it.
You sliced exactly one quarter of the cake. Magnificent. Next?
Close! That slice is a bit too big for one quarter. Slice again.
Close! That slice is a bit too small for one quarter. Slice again.
Swipe down across the cake along the dotted line to slice off one quarter of it.
You sliced exactly one third of the cake. Magnificent. Next?
Close! That slice is a bit too big for one third. Slice again.
Close! That slice is a bit too small for one third. Slice again.
Swipe down across the cake along the dotted line to slice off one third of it.
You sliced exactly three quarters of the cake. Magnificent. Next?
Close! That slice is a bit too big for three quarters. Slice again.
Close! That slice is a bit too small for three quarters. Slice again.
Swipe down across the cake along the dotted line to slice off three quarters of it.
You sliced exactly one sixth of the cake. Magnificent. Next?
Close! That slice is a bit too big for one sixth. Slice again.
Close! That slice is a bit too small for one sixth. Slice again.
Swipe down across the cake along the dotted line to slice off one sixth of it.
Every cake, sliced perfectly. You really see fractions in shapes now.
Hmm, your swipe didn't cross the cake. Try a longer drag from edge to edge.
Balanced! That weighs exactly one half. You matched every one — nice work. Here's a fresh set…
Balanced! Those fractions together weigh exactly one half — equivalent. Next one…
Make the right side weigh the same as one half on the left — using other fractions.
Balanced! That weighs exactly three quarters. You matched every one — nice work. Here's a fresh set…
Balanced! Those fractions together weigh exactly three quarters — equivalent. Next one…
Make the right side weigh the same as three quarters on the left — using other fractions.
Balanced! That weighs exactly two thirds. You matched every one — nice work. Here's a fresh set…
Balanced! Those fractions together weigh exactly two thirds — equivalent. Next one…
Make the right side weigh the same as two thirds on the left — using other fractions.
Balanced! That weighs exactly one quarter. You matched every one — nice work. Here's a fresh set…
Balanced! Those fractions together weigh exactly one quarter — equivalent. Next one…
Make the right side weigh the same as one quarter on the left — using other fractions.
Perfect — 2 cookies shared between 4 friends, so everyone gets one half of a cookie. Ready for a tougher one?
You did it! 2 cookies between 4 friends is one half each. Sharing fairly IS a fraction. Brilliant.
Share 2 cookies fairly between 4 friends. Tap a cookie to cut it in half, then drag the pieces so everyone gets the same.
Perfect — 3 cookies shared between 4 friends, so everyone gets three quarters of a cookie. Ready for a tougher one?
You did it! 3 cookies between 4 friends is three quarters each. Sharing fairly IS a fraction. Brilliant.
Share 3 cookies fairly between 4 friends. Tap a cookie to cut it in half, then drag the pieces so everyone gets the same.
Close! Someone has more than the others. Every friend needs the exact same amount — tap a piece to send it back and try again.
